name: Kathmandu Durbar Square
description: Royal palace complex in central Kathmandu. A UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring Hanuman Dhoka Palace, the Taleju Temple, Kumari Ghar, and dozens of temples and courtyards built between the 12th and 18th centuries.

name: Patan Durbar Square
description: Historic royal palace complex in Lalitpur (Patan), known for its concentration of Newar architecture. Houses the Patan Museum, Krishna Mandir, and the Golden Temple. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Bhaktapur Durbar Square
description: Best-preserved medieval town center among the three Durbar Squares. Features the 55-Window Palace, Nyatapola Temple, and Vatsala Temple. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Swayambhunath
description: Ancient Buddhist stupa atop a hill west of Kathmandu. Known as the Monkey Temple. One of the oldest religious sites in Nepal, dating to the 5th century CE. Features the iconic Buddha eyes.

name: Boudhanath Stupa
description: One of the largest spherical stupas in the world. Center of Tibetan Buddhism in Nepal, surrounded by over 50 Tibetan monasteries. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Pashupatinath Temple
description: Sacred Hindu temple on the banks of the Bagmati River. Dedicated to Lord Shiva (Pashupati). One of the most important Shiva temples in the world. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Changu Narayan Temple
description: Oldest Hindu temple in the Kathmandu Valley, dating to the 4th century CE. Dedicated to Vishnu. Renowned for its early Lichhavi-era stone inscriptions and sculptures. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Lumbini
description: Birthplace of Siddhartha Gautama (Lord Buddha). Located in the Rupandehi district. Features the Mayadevi Temple, Ashoka Pillar (249 BCE), and sacred garden. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Kirtipur
description: Ancient Newar city on a hilltop southwest of Kathmandu. Known for the Bagh Bhairav Temple, Chilancho Stupa, and its fierce resistance during Prithvi Narayan Shah's unification campaign.

name: Nuwakot Durbar
description: Fortified palace complex in Nuwakot district. Built by Prithvi Narayan Shah as a strategic stronghold during the unification of Nepal. Seven-story tower with traditional architecture.

name: Thimi
description: Ancient Newar town between Kathmandu and Bhaktapur. Famous for pottery, mask-making, and the spectacular Bisket Jatra celebrations with sindoor throwing.

name: Sankhu
description: One of the oldest settlements in the Kathmandu Valley. Known for the Bajrayogini Temple and its traditional Newar architecture. Severely damaged in the 2015 earthquake.
